functions.name: XL.CopyContent
functions.description: Copies all content from one book to other book. The plugin loops through all sheets and creates matching sheets on new book. Formats and fonts are created on the fly. This allows you to convert from XLS to XLSX or back. Please report if something is not copied. Of course plugin can only copy what libXL supports, so things like movies or diagrams are not copied. And if you use demo mode, only a small part is copied with values. Rest will be blank.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: otherBookRef
parameters.description: The reference to the second workbook. Please use XL.NewBook to create a file.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: Which items to skip when copying content.

functions.name: XL.CopyRow
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.CopyRow"; bookRef1; bookRef2; sheetIndex1; sheetIndex2; SourceRow; DestRow { ; Options } )
functions.description: Copies cells from one row to another row. Source and destination can be in different sheets/books. This copies most properties and all cells. If something is missing, please let us know.

parameters.name: bookRef1
parameters.description: The reference to the source workbook. Please use XL.LoadBook to load a file.

parameters.name: bookRef2
parameters.description: The reference to the destination workbook. Please use XL.LoadBook to load a file.

parameters.name: sheetIndex1
parameters.description: The source index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: sheetIndex2
parameters.description: The destination index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: SourceRow
parameters.description: The source row index.

parameters.name: DestRow
parameters.description: The destination row index.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: Which items to skip when copying content.

examples.label: Copy row:
examples.text_code:
# load one from container
Set Variable [
$bookRef
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.LoadBook
";
XL::ExcelFile
)]
# load one from file
Set Variable [
$bookRef2
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.LoadBook
"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/Format2.xls")]
# copy a row from first sheet. Source row 2 to dest row 10.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.CopyRow";
$bookRef2
;
$bookRef
; 0; 0; 2; 10 )]
#Save to container and cleanup
Set Field [
XL::OutputFile
;
MBS
("XL.Save";
$bookRef
; "test.xls")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.Release
";
$bookRef
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.Release
";
$bookRef2
)]
Set Field [
XL::Result
;
$r
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load one from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.LoadBook"; XL::ExcelFile)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$bookRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load one from file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.LoadBook"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/Format2.xls")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$bookRef2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> copy a row from first sheet. Source row 2 to dest row 10.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.CopyRow"; $bookRef2; $bookRef; 0; 0; 2; 10 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Save to container and cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("XL.Save"; $bookRef; "test.xls")</Calculation>
    <Field table="XL" id="99999" name="OutputFile"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Book.Release"; $bookRef)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Book.Release"; $bookRef2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r</Calculation>
    <Field table="XL" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.CopySheet
functions.description: Copies a sheet to another book. This copies most properties and all cells. If something is missing, please let us know.

parameters.name: bookRef1
parameters.description: The reference to the source workbook. Please use XL.LoadBook to load a file.

parameters.name: bookRef2
parameters.description: The reference to the destination workbook. Please use XL.LoadBook to load a file.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: Which items to skip when copying content.

examples.label: Merge two Excel documents:
examples.text_code:
# Load 2 existing books
Set Variable [
$$Book_WKPI
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.LoadBook
";
SheetCopy::XLSX1
; 0 ) ]
Set Variable [
$$Book_MKPI
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.LoadBook
";
SheetCopy::XLSX2
; 0 ) ]
#
# Make sure sheets have names
Set Variable [
$mbs
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.SetName
";
$$Book_MKPI
; 0; "Monthly" ) ]
Set Variable [
$mbs
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.SetName
";
$$Book_WKPI
; 0; "Weekly" ) ]
#
# copy one to other
Set Variable [
$mbs
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.CopySheet";
$$Book_MKPI
;
$$Book_WKPI
; 0 ) ]
#
# save result in container
Set Variable [
$fileName
; Value: Substitute("Statistic__" & Substitute (Get(CurrentDate);".";"-") & "_" &  Substitute(Get(CurrentTime); ":"; "-") & ".xlsx"; " Uhr"; "") ]
Set Field [
SheetCopy::ResultXLSX
;
MBS
("
XL.Book.Save
";
$$Book_WKPI
;
$fileName
) ]
#
# save result to desktop
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: Get(DesktopPath) &
$fileName
]
Export Field Contents [
SheetCopy::ResultXLSX
; â€ś
$Path
â€ť ; Automatically open ; Create folders: Off ]
#
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.Release
";
$$Book_MKPI
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.Release
";
$$Book_WKPI
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load 2 existing books</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.LoadBook"; SheetCopy::XLSX1; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$Book_WKPI</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.LoadBook"; SheetCopy::XLSX2; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$Book_MKPI</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Make sure sheets have names</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.SetName"; $$Book_MKPI; 0; "Monthly" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$mbs</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.SetName"; $$Book_WKPI; 0; "Weekly" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$mbs</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> copy one to other</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.CopySheet"; $$Book_MKPI; $$Book_WKPI; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$mbs</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> save result in container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Substitute("Statistic__" & Substitute (Get(CurrentDate);".";"-") & "_" &  Substitute(Get(CurrentTime); ":"; "-") & ".xlsx"; " Uhr"; "") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fileName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("XL.Book.Save"; $$Book_WKPI; $fileName) </Calculation>
    <Field table="SheetCopy" id="99999" name="ResultXLSX "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> save result to desktop</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(DesktopPath) & $fileName </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="132" name="Export Field Contents">
    <CreateDirectories state="False"/>
    <AutoOpen state="False"/>
    <CreateEmail state="False"/>
    <Field table="SheetCopy" id="99999" name="ResultXLSX ; â€ś$Pathâ€ť ; Automatically open"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Book.Release"; $$Book_MKPI ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Book.Release"; $$Book_WKPI ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.Date.DoubleToTimeStamp
functions.description: Converts a timestamp number to a timestamp value. This uses current time zone.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The time, date or time stamp to convert.

examples.label: Convert a timestamp number:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Date.DoubleToTimeStamp"; $bookRef; 40579.413796)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.Date.Pack
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Date.Pack"; BookRef; Year; Month; Day { ; Hour; Minute; Second; msec } )
functions.description: Packs date and time information into double type.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: Year
parameters.description: The year value.

parameters.name: Month
parameters.description: The month value from 1 to 12.

parameters.name: Day
parameters.description: The day value.

parameters.name: Hour
parameters.description: The hour value from 0 to 23.

parameters.name: Minute
parameters.description: The minute value from 0 to 59.

parameters.name: Second
parameters.description: The second value from 0 to 59.

parameters.name: msec
parameters.description: The millisecond value from 0 to 999.

examples.label: Build a date:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Date.Pack"; $$book; 2013; 10; 21; 19; 55; 13)
// shows 41568.83001157407125</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Build a date without time:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "XL.Date.Pack";
$$book
; 2013; 10; 21)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Date.Pack"; $$book; 2013; 10; 21)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create Excel file with some date value:
examples.text_code:
#create new xls file
Set Variable [
$book
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.NewBook
"; 0 )]
#create date format
Set Variable [
$dateFormat
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.AddFormat
";
$book
)]
Set Variable [
$dateFormatID
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.AddCustomNumFormat
";
$book
; "mmmm\ d\,\ yyyy")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Format.SetNumFormat
";
$book
;
$dateFormat
;
$dateFormatID
)]
#create sheet
Set Variable [
$sheet
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.AddSheet
";
$book
; "Sales Receipt")]
#Write date
Set Variable [
$d
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Date.Pack";
$book
; 2013; 10; 21)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.CellWriteNumber
";
$book
;
$sheet
; 4; 4;
$d
;
$dateFormat
)]
#
Set Field [
Writing data::Output
;
MBS
("
XL.Book.Save
";
$book
; "test.xls")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
XL.Book.Release
";
$book
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>create new xls file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.NewBook"; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$book</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>create date format</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Book.AddFormat"; $book)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$dateFormat</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Book.AddCustomNumFormat"; $book; "mmmm\ d\,\ yyyy")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$dateFormatID</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Format.SetNumFormat"; $book; $dateFormat; $dateFormatID)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>create sheet</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Book.AddSheet"; $book; "Sales Receipt")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sheet</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Write date</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Date.Pack"; $book; 2013; 10; 21)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$d</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteNumber"; $book; $sheet; 4; 4; $d; $dateFormat)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("XL.Book.Save"; $book; "test.xls")</Calculation>
    <Field table="Writing data" id="99999" name="Output"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Book.Release"; $book)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.FormControl.SetChecked
functions.description: Sets the check state. Sets whether a check box is selected or a radio button is selected. This attribute only applies to check box and radio button form controls. Value is 0 for unchecked, 1 for checked and 2 for mixed.

parameters.description: The new setting.

examples.label: Loop over form control to find one and check the checkbox:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$sheet
; Value: 0 ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.FormControlCount
";
$bookRef
;
$sheet
) ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
If [
$index
â‰¤
$count
]
Loop
# your script steps here
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.FormControl.Name
";
$bookRef
;
$sheet
;
$index
) ]
If [
$name
= "Test" ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.FormControl.SetChecked";
$bookRef
;
$sheet
;
$index
; 1 ) ]
End If
#
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
â‰Ą
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sheet</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.FormControlCount"; $bookRef; $sheet ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $index â‰¤ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> your script steps here</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.FormControl.Name"; $bookRef; $sheet; $index ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $name = "Test" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.FormControl.SetChecked"; $bookRef; $sheet; $index; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index â‰Ą $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Sets the foreground color of the fill pattern to red to fill cell red:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "
XL.Book.GetRGBMode
";
$book
) = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Format.SetPatternForegroundColor
";
$book
;
$format
; 10) ]
Else
Set Variable [
$c
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Color.Pack
";
$book
; 255; 0; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Format.SetPatternForegroundColor
";
$book
;
$format
;
$c
) ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Format.SetFillPattern";
$book
;
$format
; 1 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS( "XL.Book.GetRGBMode"; $book) = 0 ]
   Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Format.SetPatternForegroundColor"; $book; $format; 10) ] 
Else
   Set Variable [ $c; Value: MBS( "XL.Color.Pack"; $book; 255; 0; 0) ]
   Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Format.SetPatternForegroundColor"; $book; $format; $c) ] 
End If
Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Format.SetFillPattern"; $book; $format; 1 ) ]</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.Format.SetNumFormat

$format
; Value:

( "
XL.Book.AddFormat
";
$book
)]
# use existing number format

$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Format.SetNumFormat";
$book
;
$format
; 7)]
# and use format
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.CellWriteNumber
";
$book
;
$sheet
; 5; 3; 123;
$format
)]

examples.label: Apply a new format to a column:
examples.text_code:
#create new format
Set Variable [
$format
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.AddFormat
";
$BookRef
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Format.SetNumFormat";
$BookRef
;
$format
; 7)]
#set as format for column
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.SetColumn
";
$BookRef
; 0; 6; 6; 50 ;
$format
; 0 )]
#loop over all rows
Set Variable [
$lastRow
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.GetLastRow
";
$BookRef
;
$sheet
)]
Set Variable [
$row
; Value:1]
Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.SetCellFormat
";
$BookRef
;
$sheet
;
$row
; 6;
$format
)]
#next
Set Variable [
$row
; Value:
$row
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$row
>
$lastRow
]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>create new format</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Book.AddFormat"; $BookRef )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$format</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Format.SetNumFormat"; $BookRef; $format; 7)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>set as format for column</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.SetColumn"; $BookRef; 0; 6; 6; 50 ; $format; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>loop over all rows</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.GetLastRow"; $BookRef; $sheet )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$lastRow</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$row</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.SetCellFormat"; $BookRef; $sheet; $row; 6; $format )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$row + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$row</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$row > $lastRow</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.Format.SetPatternBackgroundColor
functions.description: Sets the background color of the fill pattern. If you set value and book is in RGB mode you can use XL.ColorPack function to get a numeric value for color components. If the book is not in RGB mode, you can use color values as described in XL.Font.SetColor . This is the background color for the pattern, you can put in the background of a cell. If your pattern is stripes, the one color is the foreground one and the other the background one.

parameters.name: Book
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The index of the format in the book to target.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting.

If [
MBS
( "
XL.Book.GetRGBMode
";
$book
) = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Format.SetPatternBackgroundColor";
$book
;
$format
; 10) ]
Else
Set Variable [
$c
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Color.Pack
";
$book
; 255; 0; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Format.SetPatternBackgroundColor";
$book
;
$format
;
$c
) ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Format.SetFillPattern
";
$book
;
$format
; 1 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS( "XL.Book.GetRGBMode"; $book) = 0 ]
   Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Format.SetPatternBackgroundColor"; $book; $format; 10) ] 
Else
   Set Variable [ $c; Value: MBS( "XL.Color.Pack"; $book; 255; 0; 0) ]
   Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Format.SetPatternBackgroundColor"; $book; $format; $c) ] 
End If
Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Format.SetFillPattern"; $book; $format; 1 ) ]</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create a 75% gray pattern with blue and red dots:
examples.text_code:
# make sure we have RGB mode
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.SetRGBMode
";
$book
; 1 ) ]
#
# Define new format
Set Variable [
$format
; Value:

( "
XL.Book.AddFormat
";
$book
) ]
#
# Encode some RGB colors

$redcolor
; Value:

( "
XL.Color.Pack
";
$book
; 255; 0; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$bluecolor
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Color.Pack
";
$book
; 0; 0; 255) ]
#
# set colors for pattern
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Format.SetPatternForegroundColor
";
$book
;
$format
;
$redcolor
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Format.SetPatternBackgroundColor";
$book
;
$format
;

functions.name: XL.Format.SetPatternForegroundColor
functions.description: Sets the foreground color of the fill pattern. If you set value and book is in RGB mode you can use XL.ColorPack function to get a numeric value for color components. If the book is not in RGB mode, you can use color values as described in XL.Font.SetColor . This is the foreground color for the pattern, you can put in the background of a cell. If your pattern is stripes, the one color is the foreground one and the other the background one. Setting no foreground color and using a pattern, can lead to use of the default color (black).

parameters.name: Book
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The index of the format in the book to target.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new color value.

examples.label: Sets the foreground color of the fill pattern to red to fill cell red:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "
XL.Book.GetRGBMode
";
$book
) = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Format.SetPatternForegroundColor";
$book
;
$format
; 10) ]
Else
Set Variable [
$c
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Color.Pack
";
$book
; 255; 0; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Format.SetPatternForegroundColor";
$book
;
$format
;
$c
) ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Format.SetFillPattern
";
$book
;
$format
; 1 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS( "XL.Book.GetRGBMode"; $book) = 0 ]
   Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Format.SetPatternForegroundColor"; $book; $format; 10) ] 
Else
   Set Variable [ $c; Value: MBS( "XL.Color.Pack"; $book; 255; 0; 0) ]
   Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Format.SetPatternForegroundColor"; $book; $format; $c) ] 
End If
Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Format.SetFillPattern"; $book; $format; 1 ) ]</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.Initialize
functions.description: Initializes the XL library by loading it. You can keep the library in a database field and export it at runtime to a known location and load it from there. If you have no license yet, you can test this function by passing empty strings for name and key. If you have a license key, please pass it and we store it for unlocking the library later when loading/creating a book. For getting a license key, please visit the libXL website: https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/libxl.shtml Starting with version 4.4, you can pass just the filename of library and the plugin finds it in the Extensions folder or in FileMaker's application folder. Windows error 193 means that 64-bit FileMaker tried to load 32-bit library or 32-bit FileMaker tried to load 64-bit library. The bit level must match between app and library. For macOS the error message will tell you about wrong architecture. Starting version 9.5, the plugin will look into the same folder for the DLL as the plugin resides. And we still look for 32/64 suffixes to DLL name. If no name is provided, we look for libxl.dll/dylib/so files. If you get a crash here on MacOS, please check if crash report says Code Signature Invalid. Then you may need to code sign the dylib yourself to match the code signature of your runtime application. The dylib from MBS is normally code signed already and should work as is. Troubleshooting: If you get error 193 on Windows, you may have the wrong architecture. e.g. you got 32-bit, but you need 64-bit or vice versa. If you got a libxl.dylib on macOS, that is no code signed, you will get back an error message, that the file is not allowed to load (disallowed by OS policy). If you got a libxl.dylib on macOS, that is not notarized, you may see a gate keeper dialog telling you the library was not searched for malware and the file can't be loaded (disallowed by OS policy). If you got a libxl.so or libxl.dylib for the wrong CPU architecture, the function will return an error with e.g. saying you need x86, but have arm code or vice versa. if you managed to load a libxl.dylib with a broken code signature, you may see a crash when the plugin calls CreateBook function reporting a broken code signature. If the file path is wrong, the macOS and Linux plugins report an error message about that. On Windows you may just get error 126, which means "module not found". If the file is shorted than expected on macOS, you get an error with a message like this: slice 1 extends beyond end of file. If you overwrite the libxl.dylib file after loading it, you may get crashes with broken code signature, if old and new file are not identical. If you like to load the library automatically by script, you can refer to this json file: https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/files/LibXL/version.json This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: LibPath
parameters.description: Native path to the DLL on Windows or Dylib file on MacOS. Can be empty for default file name with file in same folder as plugin.

parameters.name: LicenseName
parameters.description: The name of your libXL license.

parameters.name: LicenseKey
parameters.description: The license key of your libXL license.

If [
MBS
( "
XL.IsInitialized

# iOS

# Windows

; Value: "libxl.dll"]

$key
; Value: "windows-32213..."]
Else
# Mac

$path
; Value: "libxl.dylib"]

$key
; Value: "mac-32213..."]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Initialize";
$path
; "test";
$key
)]
If [
$r
â‰  "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog [ "LibXL failed to initialize";
$r
]
End If
End If

functions.name: XL.IsInitialized
functions.description: Checks if libXL library has been initialized. If value is 1, everything is okay. If value is zero, the library is not initialized and you need to call XL.Initialize function. On a hosted database this function is better than using a global variable, as the variable can't see if initialization was made on server or client side.

$key
; Value: "win-32213..."]

$path

$key
; Value: "mac-32213..."]

XL.Initialize
";
$path
; "test";
$key
)]

â‰  "OK"]

$r
]

functions.name: XL.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of current XL books. Returns IDs for all XL books in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call XL.Book.Release in your scripts. Normally after all scripts are done, this list should be empty.

examples.label: List all object IDs:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.List" ) ]

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.LoadBook
functions.description: Loads a Excel file. Works with both XML based xlsx files and older xls files. Book reference numbers are starting at 19000 and counting up for each new book. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: Either text with file path or container value which contains the Excel file data.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Which type of file this is. Pass 0 for unknown, 1 to try xml format (xlsx) and 2 to try binary format (xls). Default is 0 to detect automatically.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: Optional password for reading password protected files. Requires LibXL 5.0.

functions.result: Returns book reference number on success or error.

examples.label: Load file from container with auto detect:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$book
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.LoadBook";
MyTable::myFileContainer
; 0 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.LoadBook"; MyTable::myFileContainer; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$book</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Preprocess file before import in FileMaker by removing a row and a sheet:
examples.text_code:
# Find libXL and load it
If [
MBS
("
XL.IsInitialized
")  â‰  1 ]
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; â€śInitXLâ€ť ; Parameter:    ]
End If
#
# path to xls or xlsx file
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
MBS
( "
Folders.UserDesktop
" ); "test.xls" ) ]
#
# Load template excel file
Set Variable [
$bookRef
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.LoadBook";
$path
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error" ;
$bookRef
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
#
# Remove first row of first sheet
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.RemoveRow
";
$BookRef
; 0 /* sheet index */; 0 /* row index */) ]
#
# Remove second sheet
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.DeleteSheet
";
$bookRef
; 1 /* sheet index */ ) ]
#
# Save to file and cleanup
Set Field [
XL::OutputFile
;
MBS
("
XL.Book.SaveToFile
";
$bookRef
;
$path
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.Release
";
$bookRef
) ]
#
# now let FileMaker import file

functions.name: XL.LoadBookInfo
functions.description: Loads a Excel file metadata. Loads only information about sheets. After loading, you may check XL.Book.SheetCount and XL.Book.SheetName for the sheet names. Works with both XML based xlsx files and older xls files. Book reference numbers are starting at 19000 and counting up for each new book.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: Either text with file path or container value which contains the Excel file data.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Which type of file this is. Pass 0 for unknown, 1 to try xml format (xlsx) and 2 to try binary format (xls). Default is 0 to detect automatically.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: Optional password for reading password protected files. Requires LibXL 5.0.

functions.result: Returns book reference number on success or error.

examples.label: Load sheet metadata:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$bookRef
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.LoadBookInfo"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.xlsx") ]

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$bookRef</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.LoadBookPartially
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.LoadBookPartially"; Data; SheetIndex; FirstRow; LastRow { ; Type; keepAllSheets; Password } )
functions.description: Loads a Excel file partially. Loads a file only with specified sheet index and row range into memory. Works with both XML based xlsx files and older xls files. Book reference numbers are starting at 19000 and counting up for each new book.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: Either text with file path or container value which contains the Excel file data.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet to delete. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The first row index to load. Starts with 0 for first one.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The last row index to load.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Which type of file this is. Pass 0 for unknown, 1 to try xml format (xlsx) and 2 to try binary format (xls). Default is 0 to detect automatically.

parameters.name: keepAllSheets
parameters.description: If this is true, all sheets are saved back with XL.Book.Save function, But only one specified sheet is available for processing, thus LibXL can save more memory. It works only for xlsx files, LibXL ignores this flag for xls files.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: Optional password for reading password protected files. Requires LibXL 5.0.

functions.result: Returns book reference number on success or error.

examples.label: Try partial loading:
examples.text_code:
# Load second sheet
Set Variable [
$bookRef
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.LoadBookPartially";
XL::ExcelFile
; 1; 0; 100) ]
Set Field [
XL::Result
;
$bookRef
]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error" ;
$bookRef
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
# First row
# Save to container and cleanup
Set Field [
XL::OutputFile
;
MBS
("XL.Save";
$bookRef
; "test.xlsx") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.Release
";
$bookRef
) ]
Set Field [
XL::Result
;
$r
]
examples.xml_code:

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load second sheet</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$bookRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$bookRef </Calculation>
    <Field table="XL" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Error" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$bookRef </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> First row</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Save to container and cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("XL.Save"; $bookRef; "test.xlsx") </Calculation>
    <Field table="XL" id="99999" name="OutputFile "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Book.Release"; $bookRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    <Field table="XL" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.Sheet.AddConditionalFormatting
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Sheet.AddConditionalFormatting"; BookRef; SheetIndex; rowFirst; rowLast; ColFirst; ColLast )
functions.description: Adds a conditional formatting rules to the sheet. Requires LibXL 4.1 and only for xlsx files. With version 5.1 you need to pass a rectangle here for the first range.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: rowFirst
parameters.description: The first row for the area. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: rowLast
parameters.description: The last row for the area. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: ColFirst
parameters.description: The first column for the area. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: ColLast
parameters.description: The last column for the area. First column has index 0.

functions.result: Returns conditional formatting index number or error.

";
$book

XL.ConditionalFormat.SetFillPattern
";
$book
;
$cFormat
; 1 /* solid */) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

("
XL.ConditionalFormat.SetPatternBackgroundColor
";
$book
;
$cFormat
;
MBS
("
XL.Color.Pack
";
$book
; 240; 240; 240)) ]
#
Set Variable [
$
CF
; Value:
MBS
("XL.Sheet.addConditionalFormatting";
$book
;
$sheet
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

("
XL.ConditionalFormating.AddRange
";
$book
;
$
CF
; 4; 20; 1; 10) ]

;
$
CF
; 6 /* Expression */;

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Sheet.addConditionalFormatting"; $book; $sheet) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

functions.name: XL.Sheet.AddDataValidation
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Sheet.AddDataValidation"; BookRef; SheetIndex; Type; Operator; rowFirst; rowLast; ColFirst; ColLast; Value1; Value2 { ; allowBlank; hideDropDown; showInputMessage; showErrorMessage; promptTitle; Prompt; errorTitle; error; errorStyle } )
functions.description: Adds a data validation for the specified range. Only for xlsx files. If value1 and value2 are numbers, we call xlSheetAddDataValidationDoubleW/xlSheetAddDataValidationDoubleExW in LibXL for double/date comparison. Otherwise we call xlSheetAddDataValidationW/xlSheetAddDataValidationExW with text. If you pass more than 12 parameters, we call the Ex variants of the functions and pass those extra parameters.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet to delete. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Number for the type of data validation.

parameters.name: Operator
parameters.description: Number for the relational operator of data validation.

parameters.name: rowFirst
parameters.description: The first row for the area. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: rowLast
parameters.description: The last row for the area. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: ColFirst
parameters.description: The first column for the area. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: ColLast
parameters.description: The last column for the area. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: Value1
parameters.description: The first value for relational operator, use double quotes if you want to specify a list of values directlyâ€¨(for example "A,B,C") and don't use quotes if you want to specify a reference to area with valuesâ€¨(for example A1:A6);

parameters.name: Value2
parameters.description: The second value for VALIDATION_OP_BETWEEN or VALIDATION_OP_NOTBETWEEN operator;â€¨allowBlank - a boolean value indicating whether the data validation treats empty or blank entries as valid, 'true' means empty entries are OK and do not violate the validation constraints;

parameters.name: allowBlank
parameters.description: A boolean value indicating whether the data validation treats empty or blank entries as valid, 'true' means empty entries are OK and do not violate the validation constraints. Default is 1.

parameters.name: hideDropDown
parameters.description: A boolean value indicating whether to display the dropdown combo box for a list type data validation (VALIDATION_TYPE_LIST) Default is 0.

parameters.name: showInputMessage
parameters.description: A boolean value indicating whether to display the input prompt message Default is 1.

parameters.name: showErrorMessage
parameters.description: a boolean value indicating whether to display the error alert message when an invalid value has been entered, according to the criteria specified Default is 1.

parameters.name: promptTitle
parameters.description: Title bar text of input prompt.

parameters.name: Prompt
parameters.description: message text of input prompt

parameters.name: errorTitle
parameters.description: title bar text of error alert

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: message text of error alert

parameters.name: errorStyle
parameters.description: Number for the style of error alert used for this data validation. Default is 0 for stop error style.

functions.name: XL.Sheet.AddIgnoredError
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Sheet.AddIgnoredError"; BookRef; SheetIndex; rowFirst; ColFirst; rowLast; ColLast; ignoreErrors )
functions.description: Adds the ignored error for specified range. It allows to hide green triangles on left sides of cells. For example, if a cell is formatted as text but contains a numeric value, this is considered to be a potential error because the number won't be treated as a number, for example, in calculations. It is possible to combine a few IgnoredError values as list. Returns error message on failure.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: rowFirst

parameters.name: ColFirst
parameters.description: The first column for the area. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: rowLast
parameters.description: The last row for the area. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: ColLast
parameters.description: The last column for the area. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: ignoreErrors
parameters.description: The combination of errors to ignore.

        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Add filter with a list of values:
examples.text_code:
# AUTO FILTER: :: Applies the AutoFilter to the sheet
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.AutoFilter
";
$book
;
$sheet
) ]
#
# Select Cells :: Sets the cell range of AutoFilter with header.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.AutoFilter
.SetRef";
$book
;
$sheet
;
$headerRow
;
$lastRow
; 4; 4 ) ]
#
# Add Filter :: Adds the filter values from a values list.
Set Variable [
$filterCount
; Value: ValueCount (
$FilterValues
) ]
Set Variable [
$filterIndex
; Value: 1 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$filterValue
; Value: GetValue(
$filterValues
;
$filterIndex
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.AutoFilter
.AddFilter";
$book
;
$sheet
; 0;
$filterValue
) ]
#
# next
Set Variable [
$filterIndex
; Value:
$filterIndex
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$filterIndex
>
$filterCount
]
End Loop
#
# Apply Filter :: Applies the AutoFilter to the sheet
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.ApplyFilter
";
$book
;
$sheet
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> AUTO FILTER: :: Applies the AutoFilter to the sheet</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.AutoFilter"; $book; $sheet) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Select Cells :: Sets the cell range of AutoFilter with header.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.AutoFilter.SetRef"; $book; $sheet; $headerRow; $lastRow; 4; 4 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add Filter :: Adds the filter values from a values list.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount ( $FilterValues ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filterCount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filterIndex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($filterValues; $filterIndex) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filterValue</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.AutoFilter.AddFilter"; $book; $sheet; 0; $filterValue) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$filterIndex + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filterIndex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $filterIndex > $filterCount </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Apply Filter :: Applies the AutoFilter to the sheet</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.ApplyFilter"; $book; $sheet) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.description: Sets the custom filter criteria. Operator1: operator used by the filter comparison in the first filter criteria;â€¨Value1: value used in the first filter criteria;â€¨Operator2: operator used by the filter comparison in the second filter criteria;â€¨Value2: value used in the second filter criteria;â€¨AndOperator: Flag indicating whether the two criteria have an "and" relationship.

parameters.name: BookRef

parameters.name: SheetIndex

parameters.name: FilterColumnIndex
parameters.description: The column index of the filter. Range from 0 to XL.Sheet.AutoFilter .ColumnSize-1.

parameters.name: Operator1
parameters.description: The operator used by the filter comparison in the first filter criteria. Can be =, â‰ , <, â‰¤, > or â‰Ą

parameters.name: Value1
parameters.description: value used in the first filter criteria

parameters.name: Operator2
parameters.description: The operator used by the filter comparison in the second filter criteria. Can be =, â‰ , <, â‰¤, > or â‰Ą

parameters.name: Value2
parameters.description: The value used in the second filter criteria.

parameters.name: AndOperator
parameters.description: Flag indicating whether the two criterias have an "and" relationship. Pass "And" or "Or".

;

;
$sheet

;

parameters.name: BookRef

parameters.name: SheetIndex

examples.label: Write a formula with result:
examples.text_code:
# calculate the address like A1 and B1
Set Variable [
$a1
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.RowColToAddr
";
$book
;
$sheet
;
$row
; 1; 0; 0)]
Set Variable [
$a2
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.RowColToAddr
";
$book
;
$sheet
;
$row
; 2; 0; 0)]
# build the formula
Set Variable [
$formula
; Value: "=" &
$a1
& "+" &
$a2
]
# and we calculate in result based on our data
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Sheet.CellReadNumber";
$book
;
$sheet
;
$row
; 1 ) +
MBS
( "XL.Sheet.CellReadNumber";
$book
;
$sheet
;
$row
; 2 ) ]
# write it to the new cell
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulaBoolean
";
$book
;
$sheet
;
$Row
;
$Column
;
$formula
;
$result
;
$format
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> calculate the address like A1 and B1</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.RowColToAddr"; $book; $sheet; $row; 1; 0; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$a1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.RowColToAddr"; $book; $sheet; $row; 2; 0; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$a2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> build the formula</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"=" & $a1 & "+" & $a2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$formula</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and we calculate in result based on our data</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellReadNumber"; $book; $sheet; $row; 1 ) + MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellReadNumber"; $book; $sheet; $row; 2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write it to the new cell</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulaBoolean"; $book; $sheet; $Row; $Column; $formula; $result; $format </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Try the function:
examples.text_code:
# Initialize the
XL
functions if not already initialized
If [
MBS
("
XL.IsInitialized
") â‰  1]
Set Variable [
$libPath
; Value: "/Library/FileMaker Server/libxl.dylib"]

$initResult
; Value:
MBS
("
XL.Initialize
";

)]
End If
# Create a new Excel workbook
Set Variable [
$book
; Value:

("

"; 0)]

; Value:

";

; "TestSheet")]

";

;

; 0; 0; 123)]

";

;

; 0; 1; "Hello World")]

";

; 0; 3; "=SUM(A1)")]

& "Â¶B1: " &

& "Â¶C1: " &

& "Â¶D1: " &

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Initialize the XL functions if not already initialized</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("XL.IsInitialized") â‰  1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Library/FileMaker Server/libxl.dylib"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$libPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Initialize"; $libPath)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$initResult</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Create a new Excel workbook</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.NewBook"; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$book</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add a sheet named "TestSheet"</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Book.AddSheet"; $book; "TestSheet")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sheet</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write a number to cell A1</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Sheet.CellWriteNumber"; $book; $sheet; 0; 0; 123)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write a text to cell B1</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Sheet.CellWriteText"; $book; $sheet; 0; 1; "Hello World")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write a boolean to cell C1</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Sheet.CellWriteBoolean"; $book; $sheet; 0; 2; True)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write a formula to cell D1</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormula"; $book; $sheet; 0; 3; "=SUM(A1)")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Query the type of each cell</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Sheet.CellType"; $book; $sheet; 0; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$typeA1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Sheet.CellType"; $book; $sheet; 0; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$typeB1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Sheet.CellType"; $book; $sheet; 0; 2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$typeC1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Sheet.CellType"; $book; $sheet; 0; 3)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$typeD1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Display the results</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Cell Types"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"A1: " & $typeA1 & "Â¶B1: " & $typeB1 & "Â¶C1: " & $typeC1 & "Â¶D1: " & $typeD1</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Save the workbook to a container field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("XL.Book.Save"; $book; "TestWorkbook.xls")</Calculation>
    <Field table="YourTable" id="99999" name="ExcelFile"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Release the workbook to free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XL.Book.Release"; $book)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.Sheet.CellWriteBooleans
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteBooleans"; BookRef; SheetIndex; First row; Last row; First column; Last column; Values { ; Row Delimiter; Column Delimiter; DoBlanksForEmptyText } )
functions.description: Writes booleans to an area of cells. You can write one value or multiple values to a given range of cells. Returns number of errors occurred, so on success you get back zero.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: First row
parameters.description: The first row to process. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: Last row
parameters.description: The last row to process. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: First column
parameters.description: The first column to process. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: Last column
parameters.description: The last column to process. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: The values to use. Can be separated with row and column delimiter to give each row and column a different value. But you can also use same value for all rows or for all columns.

parameters.name: Row Delimiter
parameters.description: The row delimiter for the result. Default is new line character.

parameters.name: Column Delimiter
parameters.description: The column delimiter for the result. Default is tab character.

parameters.name: DoBlanksForEmptyText
parameters.description: Whether to write blank cells. If you pass an empty value for a cell, we can either make it a text cell with empty text or mark it as an empty cell with no value.

functions.result: Returns number of errors or error.

functions.name: XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormula
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormula"; BookRef; SheetIndex; Row; Column; Formula { ; Format } )
functions.description: Writes a formula into cell. As LibXL does not calculate the result of the formula, it will not encode the result in the Excel file. It does only store the formula, so an app opening the file will have to do the calculation. But QuickLook on macOS does not calculate, so results do not show there. See also XL.Sheet.CellReadFormula and XL.Sheet.CellIsFormula . Examples for formulas are: "TODAY()", "IF(C1>0;ABS(C1*D1);"""")", "SUM(E16:E38)" or "E39+E39*E40". See also XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulaBoolean , XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulaNumber and XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulaText where you can provide the result of the calculation as most viewer don't calculate and just show the stored value. Excel will perform missing calculations when you open the document.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: Row
parameters.description: The row for the cell. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The column for the cell. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: Formula
parameters.description: The new formula to write.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The format for the cell. This is index from 0 to XL.GetFormatCount-1 in the book's list of formats.

examples.label: Writes a formula with bold font format:
examples.text_code:
# add a bold font based on another font
Set Variable [
$textFontBold
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.AddFont
";
$book
;
$textFont
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Font.SetBold
";
$book
;
$textFontBold
; 1) ]
# add format based on other format
Set Variable [
$dollarFormatBold
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.AddFormat
";
$book
;
$dollarFormat
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Format.SetFont
";
$book
;
$dollarFormatBold
;
$textFontBold
) ]
# use format
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormula";
$book
;
$sheet
; 38; 4; "SUM(E16:E38)";
$dollarFormatBold
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add a bold font based on another font</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Book.AddFont"; $book; $textFont) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$textFontBold</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Font.SetBold"; $book; $textFontBold; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add format based on other format</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Book.AddFormat"; $book; $dollarFormat) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$dollarFormatBold</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Format.SetFont"; $book; $dollarFormatBold; $textFontBold) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> use format</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormula"; $book; $sheet; 38; 4; "SUM(E16:E38)"; $dollarFormatBold) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulaBoolean
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulaBoolean"; BookRef; SheetIndex; Row; Column; Formula; Value { ; Format } )
functions.description: Writes a formula and value in a cell. The value should be the current result, so there is something to display without calculating.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: Row
parameters.description: The row for the cell. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The column for the cell. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: Formula
parameters.description: The new formula to write.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The boolean value to put in cell.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The format for the cell. This is index from 0 to XL.GetFormatCount-1 in the book's list of formats.

functions.name: XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulaNumber
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulaNumber"; BookRef; SheetIndex; Row; Column; Formula; Value { ; Format } )
functions.description: Writes a formula and value in a cell. The value should be the current result, so there is something to display without calculating.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: Row
parameters.description: The row for the cell. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The column for the cell. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: Formula
parameters.description: The new formula to write.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The number value to put in cell.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The format for the cell. This is index from 0 to XL.GetFormatCount-1 in the book's list of formats.

functions.name: XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulaText
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulaText"; BookRef; SheetIndex; Row; Column; Formula; Value { ; Format } )
functions.description: Writes a formula and value in a cell. The value should be the current result, so there is something to display without calculating.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: Row
parameters.description: The row for the cell. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The column for the cell. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: Formula
parameters.description: The new formula to write.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The text value to put in cell.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The format for the cell. This is index from 0 to XL.GetFormatCount-1 in the book's list of formats.

functions.name: XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulas
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteFormulas"; BookRef; SheetIndex; First row; Last row; First column; Last column; Values { ; Row Delimiter; Column Delimiter } )
functions.description: Writes formulas to an area of cells. You can write one value or multiple values to a given range of cells. Returns number of errors occurred, so on success you get back zero.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: First row
parameters.description: The first row to process. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: Last row
parameters.description: The last row to process. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: First column
parameters.description: The first column to process. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: Last column
parameters.description: The last column to process. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: The values to use. Can be separated with row and column delimiter to give each row and column a different value. But you can also use same value for all rows or for all columns.

parameters.name: Row Delimiter
parameters.description: The row delimiter for the result. Default is new line character.

parameters.name: Column Delimiter
parameters.description: The column delimiter for the result. Default is tab character.

functions.result: Returns number of errors or error.

functions.name: XL.Sheet.CellWriteMatrix
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteMatrix"; BookRef; SheetIndex; MatrixRef; First row; Last row; First column; Last column )
functions.description: Writes cell values from matrix. The matrix can have numbers, texts, time, date or timestamps. You may need to use XL.Sheet.SetCellFormats after this to assign the formats for cells.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: MatrixRef
parameters.description: The matrix reference number.

parameters.name: First row
parameters.description: The first row to process. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: Last row
parameters.description: The last row to process. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: First column
parameters.description: The first column to process. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: Last column
parameters.description: The last column to process. First column has index 0.

functions.result: Returns number of errors or error.

functions.name: XL.Sheet.CellWriteNumber
functions.signature: MBS( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteNumber"; BookRef; SheetIndex; Row; Column; Value { ; Format } )
functions.description: Writes a number into cell. If you run into problem with floating point number's limited precision, you may use XL.Sheet.CellWriteTextAsNumber instead.

parameters.name: BookRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the workbook.

parameters.name: SheetIndex
parameters.description: The index of the sheet. This number ranges from 0 to XL.Book.SheetCount -1.

parameters.name: Row
parameters.description: The row for the cell. First row has index 0.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The column for the cell. First column has index 0.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value to write.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The format for the cell. This is index from 0 to XL.GetFormatCount-1 in the book's list of formats.

examples.label: Create Excel file with some date value:
examples.text_code:
#create new xls file
Set Variable [
$book
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.NewBook
"; 0 )]
#create date format
Set Variable [
$dateFormat
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.AddFormat
";
$book
)]
Set Variable [
$dateFormatID
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.AddCustomNumFormat
";
$book
; "mmmm\ d\,\ yyyy")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Format.SetNumFormat
";
$book
;
$dateFormat
;
$dateFormatID
)]
#create sheet
Set Variable [
$sheet
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Book.AddSheet
";
$book
; "Sales Receipt")]
#Write date
Set Variable [
$d
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Date.Pack
";
$book
; 2013; 10; 21)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "XL.Sheet.CellWriteNumber";
$book
;
$sheet
; 4; 4;
$d
;
$dateFormat
)]
#
Set Field [
Writing data::Output
;
MBS
("
XL.Book.Save
";
$book
; "test.xls")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
XL.Book.Release
";
$book
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XL.NewBook"; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$book</Name>

    </Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
